checks.finding: design.md ## Architecture (lines 27-56) provides a mermaid flowchart showing component boundaries (index.ts/server.ts, auth, repo-resolve, github-client, error-normalizer) and installer integration paths into 4 existing MCP clients; describes integration with existing sdd-forge-mcp/local-env-mcp patterns and installer mechanism; and the Components table (lines 57-72) plus Layer Specifications table (lines 74-82) name technology per layer (TypeScript+MCP SDK for server, node fetch/undici for HTTP, infra-spec.md for deployment). Feature Type is api-only so no frontend layer is required; this is explicitly reasoned N/A in ux-spec.md and frontend-spec.md. No issues found.

Dependency graph from the Architecture mermaid diagram (lines 29-48): clients -> S; S -> AUTH, RESOLVE, GH; GH -> API, NORM; INST -> S, CC, CX, CU, VS. All edges are one-directional with no back-edge from any downstream node (AUTH/RESOLVE/GH/API/NORM) to S or clients. No cycle detected. No issues found.

## Data Plan (lines 100-111) specifies all three required sub-fields: Data Entities: explicitly 'none' with rationale (no persistent data, no cache in v1); Existing Data Affected: lists IDE config files (~/.cursor/mcp.json, VS Code user profile mcp.json, ~/.codex/config.toml, Claude Code user-scope config) with upsert/delete-only-owned-key behavior; Migration Strategy: explicitly 'not required' (new feature) with test-backed rationale (AC-016/AC-018 preserve other entries, corrupt JSON left untouched per ADR-0005 failsafe). No issues found.

## API / Contract Plan (lines 113-165) lists all 5 new MCP tools (list_workflow_runs, get_workflow_run, list_run_jobs, get_job_log, list_run_artifacts) with input parameters and response schema shape for each, plus a full upstream-HTTP-status-to-error-code mapping table (lines 144-151) and the envelope/error-code contract (10 total codes). No existing endpoints are deprecated or breaking-changed, consistent with requirements.md Non-goals (new feature, no prior ci-mcp API existed). No issues found.

## Security Boundaries (lines 192-200) names 3 trust boundaries (B1 client<->stdio server, B2 server<->GitHub Actions API, B3 installer<->IDE config files), states the auth/authz mechanism at each (OS-user boundary for B1, env read-only PAT + GET-only for B2, user-permission scoped upsert for B3), states data classification (internal / internal-with-untrusted-upstream-output), and lists OWASP concerns per boundary (Injection, Broken Access Control/SSRF/credential leak/DoS, data destruction). security-spec.md#trust-boundaries is cross-referenced for full detail and independently covers a complete OWASP mapping table (Broken Access Control, Sensitive Data Exposure, Injection, SSRF, Security Misconfiguration, Vulnerable Components, Identification & Auth Failures). No missing item found.

SKIP: Feature Type is api-only (design.md line 4: 'Feature Type: api-only (read-only MCP server; no frontend/UI)'). ux-spec.md and frontend-spec.md both reason N/A for UI/component-tree/state sections, consistent with the declared feature type.

## Test Strategy (lines 167-190) covers unit scope (contract/schema validation via ajv against contracts/ci-mcp-tools.v1.schema.json), integration scope (fake GitHub API stubbing per tool and error-path branch), acceptance-criteria verification (explicit AC-001..AC-018 mapping throughout), and names concrete planned evidence artifacts: static-check test dirs (mcp/ci-mcp/tests/no-write/, tests/readonly/), no-secrets canary grep, MCP Inspector CLI smoke test (AC-015), installer harness files (tests/install.tests.sh/.ps1), and CI job names in .github/workflows/test.yml (typecheck/test/dist-parity). As an api-only feature with no UI, no end-to-end user-journey path is required beyond the MCP Inspector smoke test, which is present. No issues found.

All components referenced in ## Architecture (index.ts/S, auth/AUTH, repo-resolve/RESOLVE, github-client/GH, error-normalizer/NORM, install.sh/install.ps1/INST) and ## API / Contract Plan (repo-resolve) are defined in the ## Components table (lines 57-72) or as named files with responsibility descriptions. The diagram's 'error-normalizer/NORM' node is not a separate Components-table row, but its responsibility ('上流エラー正規化', upstream error normalization) is explicitly folded into the github-client.ts row description ('GET 専用 fetch ラッパ・上流エラー正規化...'), so the referenced function is defined, not left undefined. No component name is referenced without a corresponding definition or established-system-component identity (github-mcp-server, Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, VS Code Copilot are all established external systems named in requirements.md Target Users).

## ADR Change Log (lines 94-98) lists ADR-0006 (ci-mcp read-only GitHub Actions boundary decision), which exists at docs/adr/0006-ci-mcp-readonly-github-actions.md. ADR-0003 is also referenced (Technical Summary line 13, Deployment/CI Plan lines 204/212/224) and exists at docs/adr/0003-mcp-dist-bundle-distribution.md. Both referenced ADR files were confirmed present on disk. No issues found.
